LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARLOS FAJARDO, AND, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S DANGEROUS CONDITION CLAIM. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON REMAINING ISSUES DENIED. DEFENDANTS SHIFT BURDEN. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DRIVER ACTED NEGLIGENTLY WHEN HE HIT DECEDENT BASED ON THE CROSLEY AND JONES DECLARATIONS. ALL OBJECTIONS OVERRULED, ALTHOUGH AUDIO EVIDENCE NOT CONSIDERED. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. (RULING WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO THE 3RD C/A - MAY BE BROUGHT BEFORE THE TRIAL JUDGE.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Continued To Mar-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2010 Continued To May-18-10 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Apr-06-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Christopher B. Dolan, Esq. and John Roach, Esq. appeared for petitioner Lavenia Crosley, the Guardian ad Litem for the minor Antonisha Crosley.
Declaration of Lavenia Crosley in Support of the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor was filed today, April 6, 2010.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Settlement Amount - $625,000.00.
Attorney's Fees - $250,000.00.
Costs - $7,986.96.

The net balance of $367,013.04 shall be deposited into the Crosley Quality Settlement Fund, and used to purchase an annuity from Allstate Life Insurance Company as described in the attachment to the Order Approving the Compromise.

Order Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Bryan D. Lamb, Esq. appeared for petitioner Lavenia Crosley, who was not present at today's hearing.
Nicholas J. Coccimiglio, a licensed financial advisor, was placed under oath and examined.

The Court cannot proceed with today's hearing without testimony from petitioner, or a declaration from petitioner in lieu of live testimony. Good cause appearing, this matter is CONTINUED to March 25, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218. Mr. Coccimiglio appearance is waived at the next calling of the case.

Ntc Of Defts Mtn For Summ Judgment Or In The Alt Mtn For Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CARLOS FAJARDO, and, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication. Appearances: Bryan Lamb, Esq. of The Dolan Law Firm, (415) 421-2800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Blake P. Loebs, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3868, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary adjudication granted as to plaintiff's dangerous condition claim. Motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication on remaining issues denied. Defendants shift burden. Triable issue of fact raised whether driver acted negligently when he hit decedent based on the Crosley and Jones declarations. All objections overruled, although audio evidence not considered. Request for sanctions denied. (Ruling without prejudice as to the 3rd C/A - may be brought before the trial Judge.)(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
